text.title: Focus Groups Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed when applying focus-groups; each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Recommendations cite themes from one group; PM ships based on a single 8-person snapshot.
antipattern.fix: Themes require >=2 groups; isolated themes are observations not recommendations.

antipattern.symptom: Loud participant frames every topic; quiet participants nod along; output reflects 1-2 voices not 8.
antipattern.fix: 60-sec written per topic before discussion; moderator surfaces written-first opinions.

antipattern.symptom: Participants describe their problem using the concept's branded language; native vocabulary lost.
antipattern.root-cause: Concept revealed at minute 5 instead of minute 50.
antipattern.fix: Concept reveal in final 25%; vocabulary mapping happens first.
